title: WooCommerce checkout create_order idempotency evidence matrix

reusable_capabilities.title: Reusable Stripe gateway plugin profile capability
reusable_capabilities.guidance: Real Stripe checkout coverage should reuse the existing WooCommerce + WooCommerce Stripe mounting and dependency abstractions from the Stripe ECE/product-page rig track. Treat Stripe as a reusable gateway-plugin profile capability for WooCommerce workloads, not as a one-off checkout-matrix setup path.

runs.label: Old PR shape baseline
runs.expected: Fails known guardrails: public create_order writes order_awaiting_payment; legacy coupon independence is not preserved; true concurrent behavior remains unproven. Do not use this as passing evidence.

runs.label: Revised checkout candidate
runs.woocommerce_ref: replacement branch or revised PR candidate
runs.expected: Passes public create_order side-effect guardrails and resume behavior without overclaiming true concurrency unless the concurrent scenario passes. Any remaining failures must be scoped in the Woo PR description.

scenarios.label: Public create_order side effects
scenarios.old_fix_expected: FAIL: public WC_Checkout::create_order() sets order_awaiting_payment before payment processing.
scenarios.candidate_expected: PASS: public create_order() does not set order_awaiting_payment; payment-start paths own that write.

scenarios.label: Sequential retry and resume behavior
scenarios.old_fix_expected: Mixed: sequential reuse may appear to work only because create_order writes the session branch too early.
scenarios.candidate_expected: PASS: retry/resume uses the intended order_awaiting_payment branch after payment start while preserving carts for failure/cancel paths.

scenarios.label: True concurrent checkout behavior
scenarios.old_fix_expected: UNPROVEN/FAIL: old PR description overclaims Closes #62659 without proving the true concurrent duplicate-order path.
scenarios.candidate_expected: PASS only if duplicate_order_count is zero under real concurrent harness evidence; otherwise PR must avoid Closes #62659.

scenarios.label: No-payment and order-pay paths
scenarios.old_fix_expected: FAIL: old shape should expose no-payment/order-pay session or cart side-effect regressions if present.
scenarios.candidate_expected: PASS: no-payment and order-pay flows complete without unintended session side-effect regressions.

scenarios.label: Guest, logged-in, and customer identity cases
scenarios.old_fix_expected: FAIL if pending-order reuse crosses billing email, customer ID, or session identity boundaries.
scenarios.candidate_expected: PASS: same-identity retries reuse safely while different billing/customer/session identities do not mutate or reuse the wrong order.

scenarios.label: Coupon lifecycle
scenarios.old_fix_expected: FAIL when coupon reservation/usage state is consumed or leaked across retry, failed, completed, changed-cart, or public create_order paths.
scenarios.candidate_expected: PASS: coupons survive retry/failure/cancel lifecycle where WooCommerce expects cart restoration and independent public create_order calls remain independent.

scenarios.label: Hook sequencing and counts
scenarios.old_fix_expected: FAIL or scoped regression when hook counts show suppressed, duplicated, or misplaced checkout/resume/payment hooks.
scenarios.candidate_expected: PASS: checkout hooks fire once in expected order for create, payment, retry, failure, and cancel paths.

scenarios.label: Core gateway profiles
scenarios.old_fix_expected: FAIL or scoped regressions should be visible in order_awaiting_payment and cart-clearing metrics.
scenarios.candidate_expected: PASS across BACS, cheque, and COD without unexpected cart clearing.

scenarios.label: Real Stripe gateway profile
scenarios.old_fix_expected: Pending real Stripe dependency-provider stabilization; structured not_configured/build_failed/entrypoint_missing output is blocker evidence, not checkout pass/fail evidence. Reuse the existing WooCommerce Stripe ECE/product-page mounting abstractions instead of adding checkout-only Stripe setup.
scenarios.candidate_expected: PASS once Stripe dependency materialization is stable as a reusable gateway-plugin profile capability; structured build_failed or explicit skip with blocker #1336 is acceptable until then.
